item.title: Argentina rally around Messi ahead of World Cup opener
item.description: Argentina rally around Messi ahead of World Cup opener

item.title: State Of Origin: Nawaqanitawase to debut on the wing, Tino back in camp
item.description: State Of Origin: Nawaqanitawase to debut on the wing, Tino back in camp

item.title: Qera urges fans to rally behind Flying Fijians ahead of Nations Championship
item.description: Former Fiji Water Flying Fijians captain Akapusi Qera is calling on supporters to get behind the FIJI Water Flying Fijians as they prepare for the upcoming Nations Championship. Qera, who captained Fiji in 28 Test matches, the most by any captain in the nation's history, believes the tournament will provide plenty of excitement for players and fans alike.
item.content:encoded:
Former Fiji Water Flying Fijians captain Akapusi Qera is calling on supporters to get behind the FIJI Water Flying Fijians as they prepare for the upcoming Nations Championship.

Qera, who captained Fiji in 28 Test matches, the most by any captain in the nation's history, believes the tournament will provide plenty of excitement for players and fans alike.

With just 18 days remaining before Fiji's opening match against Wales at Cardiff City Stadium, the former Dean's Trophy-winning captain is encouraging fans to secure their tickets early to avoid disappointment.

A former student and captain of Suva Grammar School, Qera helped his team claim the Dean's Trophy in 2002.

Now based in England, he is expecting a thrilling contest when Fiji takes on Wales.

Qera says the opening fixture will be a great test for the Flying Fijians and an exciting spectacle for rugby fans around the world.

Fiji will face Wales on the 4th of July at Cardiff City Stadium in Wales, with kick-off scheduled for 6.10pm.

item.title: Elijah Just's double earns New Zealand a point against Iran
item.description: Elijah Just scored twice as New Zealand fought back to secure a 2-2 draw against Iran in their FIFA World Cup Group G clash.Just w

item.title: Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final sells out in 15 minutes
Rewa FC stands by player while legal process continues, urges public to avoid speculation
item.description: Rewa FC has confirmed that a former Fiji and district level football player, charged in relation to sexual offences has joined the club. In a statement, the club acknowledged that the player has been charged by Police in relation to an indictable offence and that the matter is currently before the courts.
item.content:encoded:
Rewa FC has confirmed that a former Fiji and district level football player, charged in relation to sexual offences has joined the club.

In a statement, the club acknowledged that the player has been charged by Police in relation to an indictable offence and that the matter is currently before the courts.

Rewa FC says it will not comment further on the case as legal proceedings are ongoing.

The club stressed that under the law, every person is presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law and said that the player is entitled to the same fairness, dignity and due process.

Rewa says it will continue to support him as a member of its football family while respecting the independence of the court process.

The Delta Tigers have also called on fans, supporters, media and the wider football community to avoid speculation or the spread of unverified information while the matter is before the courts.

Rewa says its focus remains on supporting its players, upholding the values of the club and respecting the rule of law.

Meanwhile, the team is preparing for the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT, where it will begin its campaign against Navua at 5pm this Friday at Subrail Park.

The team will then take on Suva at 2pm on Saturday before facing defending champions Labasa at 3pm on Sunday.

The pool matches will be played from Friday to Sunday, before the competition returns to Subrail Park next weekend for the semi-finals and final.

item.title: Uruguay fight back to earn 1-all draw against Saudi Arabia
item.description: Uruguay will take on Cape Verde next Monday at 10am before meeting Spain in their final pool match next Saturday at 12pm.
item.content:encoded: Uruguay will take on Cape Verde next Monday at 10am before meeting Spain in their final pool match next Saturday at 12pm.

item.title: All Whites chasing historic first World Cup win against Iran
item.description: The New Zealand national football team will look to create history when they take on Iran in their opening Group G match of the FIFA World Cup in Los Angeles. The All Whites are making their third FIFA World Cup appearance and are seeking their first-ever win at the tournament.
item.content:encoded:
The New Zealand national football team will look to create history when they take on Iran in their opening Group G match of the FIFA World Cup in Los Angeles.

The All Whites are making their third FIFA World Cup appearance and are seeking their first-ever win at the tournament.

Coach Darren Bazeley hopes his side can draw inspiration from the 2010 campaign, when New Zealand exited in the group stage but remained unbeaten after drawing with Slovakia, Italy and Paraguay.

However, the All Whites enter the tournament following warm-up losses to Haiti and England and are the lowest-ranked side at the World Cup.

Meanwhile, Iran captain Mehdi Taremi has criticised FIFA over the impact that tensions involving his country have had on the team's preparations for the World Cup.

Taremi says the atmosphere around the tournament has been affected and believes football should be a platform that promotes peace and unity.

He says the Iranian team was forced to relocate its training base to Mexico and that some members of the delegation and travelling supporters experienced visa and travel-related difficulties ahead of the tournament.

Taremi said he has felt the tension since arriving at the World Cup and believes the tournament could have provided a better environment for players and fans.

Despite the off-field distractions, Iran will be looking to make a strong start to their Group G campaign and strengthen their chances of reaching the knockout stages for the first time in the nation's history.

This will be only the third meeting between New Zealand and Iran, and their first in a competitive match. The teams last met in a friendly in Tehran in 2003, with Iran winning 3-0, while their first encounter ended in a 0-0 draw in Auckland in 1973.

Today's clash will also mark the first World Cup meeting between the two nations.

Meanwhile, the match between Saudi Arabia is leadingUruguay 1-0 at half time.

In other results, debutants Cape Verde held Spain to a nil-all draw, while Belgium came from behind to earn a 1-1 draw against Egypt.

item.title: Belgium came from behind to earn a 1-1 draw against Egypt

item.title: Masilevu helps Cakaudrove secure victory against Macuata
item.description: Former Fiji Water Flying Fijians and Fiji 7s winger, Benito Masilevu, played a key role in helping Cakaudrove secure an 18–15 vict
